briefing.incidentSummary:
A classified internal database was accessed illegally.

Suspicions point to an internal breach — someone with inside access and motive.

All systems were live. The attacker knew the layout.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Identify the culprit

› Use evidence wisely — some data may be misleading

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect to close the case

suspects.name: Ankita E
suspects.profileNotes: Had direct access to the finance workstation FIN-WS-114. Login session overlaps exactly with the time of credential export. Modified finance_report_q3.pdf at 09:31 AM.
suspects.description: Ankita E is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Dhruv A
suspects.profileNotes: Signed and deployed system_patch.exe. High system access but no evidence of finance data interaction. Patch contained an unusual outbound connection module — warrants scrutiny but not conclusive.
suspects.description: Dhruv A is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Manav R
suspects.profileNotes: Present in office during the breach window. No direct access to finance systems. Alibi partially corroborated by meeting logs.
suspects.description: Manav R is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

suspects.name: Ayon K
suspects.profileNotes: No access to finance systems. Committed code during breach window — alibi strong. Low threat level.
suspects.description: Ayon K is a person of interest in this case. Their motives and involvement remain under investigation.

items.label: Patch deployed successfully.
items.detail: Routine system message. Patch deployed by Dhruv A at 09:45 AM.
items.irrelevantReason: Standard deployment message with no link to the breach.

items.label: I noticed.
items.detail: Terse acknowledgement. Ghost user was monitoring system activity.
items.irrelevantReason: Too vague to assign to any specific suspect.

items.label: Check your finance workstation.
items.detail: Ghost directs attention to the finance workstation — FIN-WS-114. This is the exact machine used by Ankita E. Knowledge of this internal target suggests insider familiarity.

items.label: You left a door open.
items.detail: Metaphorical reference to an exploited vulnerability. Possibly referencing the patch that introduced a backdoor module.
items.irrelevantReason: Ambiguous message not conclusively linked to any suspect.

items.label: For the next phase, transfer to offshore account.
items.detail: Direct instruction to transfer funds to an offshore account. Cross-references the anomaly found in finance_report_q3.pdf pointing to ACC-4482-X.

items.label: finance_report_q3.pdf
items.detail: Modified by Ankita E at 09:31 AM. Contains three anomalies: ₹2.3 Crore transferred to offshore ACC-4482-X, unscheduled payment to Northstar Solutions, and 14 duplicate reimbursements on 14 Aug.

items.label: system_patch.exe
items.detail: Signed by Dhruv A (Admin). Contains an unusual outbound connection module — not typical for an internal patch. Warrants investigation but no direct link to financial data.
items.irrelevantReason: Suspicious but no direct link to the credential export or offshore transfer.

items.label: debug_log.txt
items.detail: Log shows unexpected privilege escalation, hash mismatch in agent.dll, and outbound connection to 185.193.127.44 at 01:14 AM. Key entry: credentials export initiated from FIN-WS-114 at 10:43 AM.

items.label: cache_dump.bin
items.detail: Contains employee credential database fragments. Dump timestamp 10:44–10:46 AM. Exfiltration path: FIN-WS-114 → 172.16.44.21.
items.irrelevantReason: Shows what was exfiltrated but not who initiated it — corroborating only.

hiddenItem.label: credentials.pdf
hiddenItem.detail: DECRYPTED — Internal Credential Export Log. Exported by Ankita E (Employee ID AE-4471) from workstation FIN-WS-114 at 10:43:22 AM. The export contains full DB admin credentials for FINDB-PROD-01 — the production financial database. The credentials were written to a portable archive and transferred via SMB to 172.16.44.21 before being routed to external IP 202.56.23.101 — an offshore server with no legitimate business relationship to the company. The export log shows 14,847 employee financial records were accessed. Most critically, the log confirms the credentials were used to execute the three anomalous transactions found in finance_report_q3.pdf — connecting Ankita E directly to the data exfiltration and the offshore account transfer. This file is the single most important piece of evidence in the case — it closes the loop between database access, credential theft, and the offshore fund transfer.

minigame.title: Unlock Hidden Clue
minigame.hint: Caesar shift by -3. Decode to unlock the filename.
hints: It's a simple shift cipher. Try moving letters backward.
The shift is exactly 3 positions back in the alphabet.

onSuccess.message: BREAKTHROUGH — credentials_export_log.pdf decrypted. Ankita E's credential export directly links her to the offshore transfer. Case evidence chain is now complete.

items.label: Device
items.detail: Workstation FIN-WS-114. Located in Finance Dept., Mumbai HQ. Access controlled by biometric + PIN.
items.irrelevantReason: Describes the machine but not who used it during the breach.

items.label: OS
items.detail: Windows 11 Pro, Build 22621. Last updated 2024-01-15. Patch KB5034123 applied.
items.irrelevantReason: OS details are not linked to any suspect or the breach.

items.label: Last User
items.detail: Last login on FIN-WS-114 was Ankita E, from 09:15 AM to 11:02 AM. This session window covers the exact time of the credential export at 10:43 AM.

items.label: Internal Origin
items.detail: Source IP 172.16.44.21 maps to FIN-WS-114 on the internal LAN. This is the originating machine for the exfiltration path found in cache_dump.bin.

items.label: External Hop
items.detail: Traffic hopped to 202.56.23.101, geolocated to a public cafe WiFi near the office. This external IP also appears in credentials.pdf as the destination of the exfiltrated data.

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Correct suspect identified with sufficient evidence chain.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

partial.title: Insufficient Evidence
partial.subtitle: Correct suspect, but not enough evidence to close the case.
partial.label: PARTIAL

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: You flagged the wrong person. The real culprit was Ankita E.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

coldCase.title: Case Gone Cold
coldCase.subtitle: No relevant evidence was collected to support the accusation.
coldCase.label: COLD CASE
